title: New Vaccines Made from Tiny DNA Shapes
summary: Scientists have created a new type of vaccine using tiny shapes made from DNA, called DNA origami. This is different from mRNA vaccines, which became famous during the COVID-19 pandemic. DNA origami vaccines are designed to help the body fight viruses like COVID-19, HIV, and Ebola. They work by showing parts of the virus to the immune system, which then learns to attack it. In tests with mice and human cells, these vaccines made strong immune responses. They are also easier to store and make than mRNA vaccines, which need very cold temperatures. Researchers hope this new technology can help protect people from many diseases in the future.

questions.question: What is the goal of DNA origami vaccines?

background_read: Vaccines are important medicines that help keep us healthy by teaching our bodies how to fight germs like viruses. During the COVID-19 pandemic, scientists made new vaccines called mRNA vaccines, which worked well but had some problems, like needing very cold storage. Now, researchers are trying to make even better vaccines using DNA origami, which are tiny shapes built from DNA. This new type of vaccine might be easier to store and could help protect against many different diseases in the future.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from Harvard University and other institutions
WHAT: Created DNA origami vaccines to fight viruses
WHERE: In labs using mice and human cells
WHY: To make better vaccines that are easier to store and produce

perspectives.description: Scientists are developing new vaccine technologies to improve health.
